title: When You Dish Upon A Star

jokes.text: Homer dreaming he's Yogi Bear: 'Hey, Bart-Bart... looks like a beautiful day to swipe some "pic-i-nic" baskets.'
jokes.setup: Homer is dreaming, speaking in an exaggerated Yogi Bear accent
jokes.punchline: The overly pronounced 'pic-i-nic' mimicking Yogi's speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons character integration - Homer's fantasy persona fits perfectly with his childlike nature, and the voice work adds layers

jokes.text: Bart as Boo-Boo: 'But, "Homi," Ranger Ned's not gonna like that.' Flanders as ranger gets mauled by bear Homer
jokes.setup: Bart warns about Ranger Ned in the Yogi Bear fantasy
jokes.punchline: Flanders gets violently mauled, subverting the wholesome cartoon premise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration putting Flanders as the authority figure who gets comeuppance, with dark comedy subverting children's cartoon expectations

jokes.text: Homer waking up: 'I was having the most wonderful dream. I had a hat and a tie, with no pants on.'
jokes.setup: Kids wake Homer from his dream
jokes.punchline: Homer's idea of wonderful is the surreal 'formal on top, naked below' combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - the combination of formal wear with nudity feels like his exact brand of confused propriety

jokes.text: Lisa: 'Actually keeping promises would make you a good father.' Homer: 'No, that would make me a great father.'
jokes.setup: Lisa corrects Homer's parenting philosophy
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and escalates his self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer missing the moral lesson entirely while inflating his ego - perfectly in character

jokes.text: 'Aw, 4:00 a.m.? Now I'll never get back to-' [immediately starts snoring]
jokes.setup: Homer complains he'll never get back to sleep
jokes.punchline: He falls asleep mid-sentence, contradicting his complaint
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection with perfect timing - the contradiction is immediate and visual

jokes.text: Homer crashes into Ned at the lake: 'Hey, Homer. Enough traffic for you?' [Homer's neck audibly cracks]
jokes.setup: Ned makes casual small talk after Homer crashes
jokes.punchline: Homer's neck making disturbing cracking sounds while trying to respond normally
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with the contrast between Ned's pleasantries and Homer's obvious injury

jokes.text: Homer: 'Hey, pfft. Screw this. Hang on, everyone. We're taking the old Simpson shortcut.'
jokes.setup: Traffic frustration builds up
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal and declaration of taking a 'shortcut' (which will obviously go wrong)
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's impulsive decision-making that will drive the plot, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Homer eating berries: 'Ugh! Pesticides. Carbamate, if I'm not mistaken. Yep. Carbamate.'
jokes.setup: Homer is eating wild berries during their shortcut
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow has expert knowledge of pesticide types despite his general ignorance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Homer's random expertise in pesticides while doing something stupid is peak Simpsons logic subversion

jokes.text: Homer: 'Way out in the sticks like this? It could only be hillbillies. So I suppose that's a hillbilly Jacuzzi. Yep. That's where they cook up their vittles.'
jokes.setup: Family sees expensive house with Jacuzzi in secluded area
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misinterpretation of obvious wealth as poverty, with confident wrongness
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident ignorance about wealth/class markers, setting up the celebrity reveal perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Couldn't put it down. Come on, boy. Let's get me a six-pack.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he read the boat safety manual
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie followed by immediate plan to drink and boat
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer recklessness with good character consistency

jokes.text: Boat attendant: 'You can't operate a boat under the influence of alcohol.' Homer: 'Oh, that sounds like a wager to me.'
jokes.setup: Official safety warning about drinking and boating
jokes.punchline: Homer interprets safety advice as a challenge to prove wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - turning safety warnings into personal challenges shows his oppositional nature perfectly

jokes.text: Homer parasailing: 'Faster, Marge! Faster. The snapping turtles are massing.'
jokes.setup: Homer is being dragged through water while parasailing
jokes.punchline: His paranoid fear of 'massing' snapping turtles adds absurd urgency
jokes.explanation: The specific image of snapping turtles 'massing' like an army is wonderfully absurd and Homer-esque

jokes.text: Apu: 'Oh, dear. You have ruined my work, you flying fat man.'
jokes.setup: Homer crashes into Apu's sand sculpture while parasailing
jokes.punchline: Apu's polite but direct description of Homer as 'flying fat man'
jokes.explanation: Apu's characteristically polite but blunt assessment fits his voice perfectly

jokes.text: Homer: 'I'm soaring majestically, like a candy wrapper caught in an updraft.'
jokes.setup: Homer is enjoying the parasailing experience
jokes.punchline: His comparison to trash blowing in the wind undercuts the majestic feeling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant poetry/bathos combination - Homer's attempt at eloquence produces a perfectly wrong metaphor that's somehow beautiful

jokes.text: Homer: 'I wanna look down on the clouds with contempt. I wanna sneer at God's creation and spit on his-'
jokes.setup: Homer gets carried away with power from being up high
jokes.punchline: His megalomania builds to blasphemous proportions before being cut off
jokes.explanation: Homer's hubris escalating to cosmic proportions is perfect character work, and the cutoff adds timing

jokes.text: Homer crashes on Kim Basinger: 'I know you. You're Kim "Basin-jer."' 'It's Basinger.'
jokes.setup: Homer crashes into famous actress Kim Basinger
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces her name despite being a 'huge fan'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer fan behavior - claims to be huge fan while getting basic facts wrong

jokes.text: Homer to Kim: 'Well, I am a married man.' Kim: 'You're crushing my husband.' Homer gasps: 'Billy Baldwin!' Alec: 'I'm Alec Baldwin.'
jokes.setup: Kim asks Homer to slide over, Homer misinterprets romantically
jokes.punchline: Homer mistakes Alec for Billy Baldwin, showing his celebrity knowledge is surface-level
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered - Homer's misplaced propriety plus celebrity confusion, typical of his character

jokes.text: 'Why don't you just move to, say, Bethesda?' 'Not phony enough.'
jokes.setup: Celebrities complain L.A. is too phony
jokes.punchline: Other cities aren't phony enough for them - they actually prefer some phoniness
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of celebrity culture - they want the right amount of artificiality, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: 'We've been living off congratulatory muffin baskets.' Homer picks up zucchini muffin: 'Blech! Zucchini.' 'Don't just put that back in the basket.' 'I'm gonna eat it later.'
jokes.setup: Celebrities explain their food situation with fancy muffin baskets
jokes.punchline: Homer's disgusting behavior putting the muffin back after tasting it
jokes.explanation: Homer's gross table manners create cringe comedy with the sophisticated celebrities

jokes.text: But you just read that card from the Gersh Agency. - I recognized the logo
jokes.setup: Alec points out Homer just read something
jokes.punchline: Homer's implausible explanation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that makes the illiteracy claim more absurd

jokes.text: Homer offering to help: 'Will you promise to keep our secret?' 'Absolutely, if you promise to keep mine.' 'What is it?' 'I can't read.' 'But you just read that card.' 'I recognized the logo.'
jokes.setup: Homer promises to keep their secret in exchange for them keeping his
jokes.punchline: Homer claims he can't read but gets caught immediately, then has an absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Homer's lie being immediately exposed and his desperate cover story

jokes.text: But you just read that card from the Gersh Agency. - I recognized the logo.
jokes.setup: Catching Homer in his lie about illiteracy
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic cover story
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Homer doubling down on an obvious lie

jokes.text: Homer to Apu: 'I'm about to purchase some weird and fruity items, and I don't want any guff.'
jokes.setup: Homer enters store with unusual confidence
jokes.punchline: He preemptively defends buying sophisticated items, showing his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Homer's defensive attitude about buying upscale items shows his awareness of how out-of-character it is

jokes.text: Homer listing fancy mushrooms: 'Portobello. Porcini. Chanterelle. And Shiitake.' Apu: 'Okay. We have none of those.'
jokes.setup: Homer confidently lists exotic mushroom varieties
jokes.punchline: Apu matter-of-factly reveals they don't carry any of these fancy items
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's newfound sophistication - reality check from small-town grocery store

jokes.text: Apu: 'These exotic items are suspiciously different from your usual order of beer and pork. What gives?'
jokes.setup: Apu notices Homer's dramatic change in shopping habits
jokes.punchline: The blunt summary of Homer's typical diet as just 'beer and pork'
jokes.explanation: Accurate character observation - Apu knows Homer well enough to spot the dramatic change

jokes.text: I'm just broadening my horizons. By the way, do you have extra-wide bumper stickers for a Humvee?
jokes.setup: Homer claims to be broadening horizons
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for celebrity-related item
jokes.explanation: Homer immediately contradicts his cover story

jokes.text: Homer polishing Oscar: 'When you win one, you can take care of it however you want.'
jokes.setup: Alec tells Homer to stop obsessively polishing the Oscar
jokes.punchline: Homer's possessive attitude toward an award that isn't his
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate sense of ownership over celebrity status symbols

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Ron Howard. - Ron Howard? - Yeah. I'm looking for Alec Baldwin and Kim Basinger.
jokes.setup: Doorbell ringing
jokes.punchline: Another major celebrity showing up
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity escalation, mostly setup for future jokes

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Ron Howard. - Ron Howard? - Yeah. I'm looking for Alec Baldwin and Kim Basinger
jokes.setup: Another celebrity appears at the door
jokes.punchline: Homer's starstruck reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple celebrity recognition gag

jokes.text: Yeah, well, it's the only town in America that'll let me fish with dynamite.
jokes.setup: Ron Howard explaining why he wants to live in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and dangerous hobby
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully unexpected character detail that explains Springfield's appeal to celebrities

jokes.text: Homer meeting Ron Howard: 'Really? You giant stars consider me a friend? Well, for the first time in my life I feel like I'm somebody.'
jokes.setup: Ron Howard refers to Homer as a friend of the celebrities
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic overjoyed reaction reveals his deep insecurity
jokes.explanation: Painfully accurate character moment - Homer's self-worth issues exposed through celebrity contact

jokes.text: Ron Howard: 'Do I smell vodka? And wheatgrass?' Homer: 'It's called a lawn mower. I invented it.'
jokes.setup: Ron notices Homer's unusual cocktail combination
jokes.punchline: Homer claims to have invented this ridiculous drink and names it poorly
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate attempt to impress through fake innovation, with a terrible drink name

jokes.text: Homer, when did you become a member of PETA? - Well, you know me. I love animals- Beef, chicken, veal.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's PETA membership
jokes.punchline: Homer listing animals he loves to eat
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectation with classic Homer logic about loving animals

jokes.text: Homer answering phone: 'Imagine Films. A division of, um, Homer Co.'
jokes.setup: Homer answers the celebrities' business phone
jokes.punchline: He makes up a fake company structure on the spot with poor improvisation
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent attempt at business sophistication falls flat with obvious improvisation

jokes.text: If you're not part of the solution, Marge, you're part of the problem. - That's not what PETA stand-
jokes.setup: Homer using activist language
jokes.punchline: Marge starting to correct his fundamental misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Homer's superficial adoption of causes he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Homer as fitness instructor: 'Keep those knees rigid. Jerk that lower back. Force it. Whip that neck.'
jokes.setup: Homer is leading an exercise routine
jokes.punchline: All his instructions are physically dangerous and wrong
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident incompetence applied to fitness - every instruction is harmful

jokes.text: Homer's movie script: 'It's about a killer-robot driving instructor who travels back in time for some reason.'
jokes.setup: Homer presents his movie script
jokes.punchline: The vague, nonsensical premise with 'for some reason' showing his lack of actual plotting
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of bad movie pitches with Homer's admission he has no real plot logic

jokes.text: Ron Howard: 'Ron Howard's attached to direct.' 'I am not.' 'Well, he expressed an interest.' 'No, I didn't.' 'Did too.' 'I did not.' 'You lie!'
jokes.setup: Homer falsely claims Ron Howard wants to direct his movie
jokes.punchline: The argument escalates into childish back-and-forth with Ron standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy with Homer digging himself deeper while the victim is present

jokes.text: 'This is only 17 pages and several of the pages are just drawings of the time machine.'
jokes.setup: Kim examines Homer's movie script
jokes.punchline: The script is extremely short and padded with drawings instead of writing
jokes.explanation: Good exposition of Homer's lazy writing - using pictures to fill space is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, if Alec is out, I'm out too. You're on your own, Potsie.
jokes.setup: Rejecting Homer's movie
jokes.punchline: Kim calling Ron Howard 'Potsie' from Happy Days
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference showing how Hollywood types view Ron Howard

jokes.text: Moe at bar: 'When was the last time Barbra Streisand cleaned out your garage? And when it's time to do the dishes, where's Ray Bolger?'
jokes.setup: Moe is defending Homer against celebrity worship
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of celebrities not helping with mundane tasks, especially dated reference to Ray Bolger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist rant with wonderfully specific and dated celebrity references for mundane tasks

jokes.text: Kent Brockman? Please. - Oh, what? I suppose you've seen a bigger star. - I might have.
jokes.setup: Barney bragging about seeing Kent Brockman
jokes.punchline: Homer's coy response hiding his bigger celebrity secret
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Homer's struggle to keep the secret while wanting to brag

jokes.text: Homer: 'Tell the people, Homer. They have a right to know about the celebrity summer house.' 'Who the hell are you?' 'What do you care? I'm telling you what you want to hear.'
jokes.setup: Homer is talking to himself in his head
jokes.punchline: He questions his own inner voice, which dismissively responds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy where Homer's conscience becomes antagonistic and self-aware - very sophisticated writing

jokes.text: Yes! In your freckled face, Howard
jokes.setup: Homer playing badminton with Ron Howard
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific insult about Ron's freckles
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in Homer's specific trash talk

jokes.text: Ron Howard playing badminton: 'My God - The lemonade. Look at the lemonade.' [Glasses shaking like Jurassic Park]
jokes.setup: Crowd is approaching the celebrity house
jokes.punchline: Ron Howard notices the approaching mob through Jurassic Park-style glass vibrations
jokes.explanation: Perfect movie parody reference with the lemonade doing the T-Rex water glass effect

jokes.text: Comic Book Guy to Kim Basinger: 'Those red pumps you wore in L.A. Confidential were fabulous. Where can I get a pair for my, uh, mother? She wears a 12 double-E.'
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy approaches Kim Basinger at the celebrity mob scene
jokes.punchline: His obviously fake story about buying women's shoes 'for his mother' with suspiciously specific sizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - obsessive movie knowledge combined with transparent personal interest

jokes.text: Where can I get a pair for my, uh, mother? She wears a 12 double-E.
jokes.setup: Asking about Kim Basinger's shoes
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about shoe size for his 'mother'
jokes.explanation: Standard cross-dressing joke with implausible cover story

jokes.text: Homer being rejected: 'But the next time you want someone to remind you which brother is which, or smell your hair while you're sleeping... just remember, old Homer won't be here anymore.'
jokes.setup: Homer is being kicked out by the celebrities
jokes.punchline: He lists extremely inappropriate 'services' he provided, especially the hair-smelling
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of boundaries revealed - the hair-smelling detail is perfectly creepy and Homer-like

jokes.text: Who is it? Is it anybody? - No, no. It's nobody. Throw your stones. It's nobody.
jokes.setup: Crowd waiting to see celebrities
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic self-deprecation to deflect the crowd
jokes.explanation: Great moment showing Homer's complete deflation and self-loathing

jokes.text: Homer at dinner: 'When Kim makes a Manwich, she uses focaccia bread. And would it kill you to put some fennel in it?'
jokes.setup: Homer is comparing Marge's cooking to Kim Basinger's
jokes.punchline: He's applying gourmet standards to Manwich, the most processed food possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect class/sophistication comedy - applying haute cuisine standards to lowest common denominator food

jokes.text: Alec Baldwin? Wow! That is the coolest person you've ever been fired by.
jokes.setup: Learning about Homer's celebrity firing
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact assessment of Homer's firing history
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that acknowledges Homer's job history while being genuinely impressed by the celebrity factor

jokes.text: Bart: 'And you kids aren't exactly John and Joan Cusack.' Lisa: 'Ankle? Focaccia? What are you talking about?' Homer: 'See? It's like we don't even speak the same language anymore.'
jokes.setup: Homer continues comparing his family unfavorably to celebrities
jokes.punchline: His brief exposure to Hollywood has made him speak like an insider, alienating his family
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's pretentiousness affecting his family relationships

jokes.text: Was it the dog from the Beethoven movies? - Of course not. - Our dog isn't famous.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning washing the dog
jokes.punchline: Lisa's hope for celebrity connection, Homer's deflation
jokes.explanation: Good showing how the celebrity obsession has infected the whole family

jokes.text: The only one who understands me is that guy who married Martha Raye
jokes.setup: Homer feels misunderstood
jokes.punchline: Obscure reference to celebrity husband
jokes.explanation: Obscure celebrity reference showing Homer's new pretensions

jokes.text: I'm about ready to ankle this family. - Ankle? Focaccia? What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Homer using Hollywood slang
jokes.punchline: Marge's confusion at his pretentious new vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good showing how Homer has adopted celebrity culture language his family doesn't understand

jokes.text: Attention, starstruck fools. Step right up and see the world's greatest mobile collection of Alec and Ron and "Kim-o-rabilia."
jokes.setup: Homer starting a roadside attraction
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'Kim-o-rabilia'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Kim's name with memorabilia, showing Homer's entrepreneurial revenge

jokes.text: Homer's celebrity memorabilia truck: 'You heard right. Twenty dollars.' Barney: 'Oh! Hundred-year-old stuff.'
jokes.setup: Homer is selling celebrity items from his truck
jokes.punchline: Barney thinks items from contemporary celebrities are 'hundred-year-old' antiques
jokes.explanation: Barney's confusion about time periods fits his character, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: 'That's right, "Mr. Tough Guy" can't handle a little penicillin.'
jokes.setup: Homer shows Alec Baldwin's medical alert bracelet
jokes.punchline: Homer mocks Baldwin's allergy as weakness despite it being a serious medical condition
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorant take on medical allergies as character flaws, showing his petty revenge

jokes.text: We can't even pay our bills, and they're drinking Royal Crown Cola.
jokes.setup: Looking at celebrity possessions
jokes.punchline: Royal Crown Cola as a symbol of celebrity excess
jokes.explanation: Funny because RC Cola is actually a cheap brand, showing misplaced resentment

jokes.text: 'When was the last time Barbra Streisand cleaned out your garage? And when it's time to do the dishes, where's Ray Bolger? I'll tell ya. Ray Bolger is lookin' out for Ray Bolger!'
jokes.setup: Homer continues his anti-celebrity rant to the crowd
jokes.punchline: The specific accusation that Ray Bolger (Scarecrow from Wizard of Oz) is selfish, building to righteous indignation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Moe's rant with Homer's righteous delivery making the absurd specific accusation even funnier

jokes.text: And we just tossed him out like a Golden Globe award.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on how they treated Homer
jokes.punchline: Comparing Homer to the less prestigious award
jokes.explanation: Sharp Hollywood insider joke about the relative value of different awards

jokes.text: Ron Howard: 'I've got to admit, I miss the way he used to tuck us in and kiss us on the forehead.' Kim: 'Forehead?'
jokes.setup: Ron Howard sentimentally remembers Homer's caretaking
jokes.punchline: Kim's shocked reaction suggests Homer kissed them somewhere other than the forehead
jokes.explanation: Good implication comedy - Kim's reaction suggests Homer's boundaries were even worse than stated

jokes.text: Discussing Homer's movie: 'What, are you crazy? It's a buddy picture. Without the pie, it would just be me on screen for two hours.' 'Oh, yeah, and you'd hate that.'
jokes.setup: They're discussing Homer's script about a robot and talking pie
jokes.punchline: Ron Howard's sarcastic response to Alec's obvious ego about screen time
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood ego satire with Ron Howard's dry response to Alec's vanity

jokes.text: Yeah. Everyone makes mistakes. I mean, we'd want another chance if one of us ever made a bad film, right? [Birds Singing]
jokes.setup: Ron Howard talking about second chances
jokes.punchline: Awkward silence after mentioning bad films
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward moment with the crickets/birds highlighting the uncomfortable silence about their bad films

jokes.text: Homer at the lake imitating Kim Basinger: 'Ooh, look at me. I'm Kim Basinger, the big movie star. I'm so beautiful. I think I'm so great. I'm too important to take Homer to the Oscars.'
jokes.setup: Homer is doing a bitter impression of Kim Basinger for his museum customers
jokes.punchline: His childish mockery reveals his hurt feelings about not getting to go to awards shows
jokes.explanation: Homer's petty revenge through mocking impressions shows his wounded ego and childish coping

jokes.text: Let's get him. - And this time it's personal. What? It is personal. He's got our underpants
jokes.setup: Standard action movie line
jokes.punchline: Alec defending the cliche because it literally applies
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about action movie cliches

jokes.text: Alec Baldwin jumping between cars: 'Uh, gee, I'd love to, but I'm not really wearing the right shoes.' [Kim makes chicken sounds]
jokes.setup: Alec needs to jump from one car to another during chase
jokes.punchline: The action star makes a lame excuse about footwear, gets mocked with chicken noises
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of action movie heroics with mundane excuses and relationship dynamics

jokes.text: Homer in court: 'I believe that famous people have a debt to everyone. If celebrities didn't want people pawing through their garbage and saying they're gay, they shouldn't have tried to express themselves creatively.'
jokes.setup: Homer gives his defense speech in court
jokes.punchline: His logic that artistic expression justifies invasion of privacy and false accusations
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of paparazzi culture with Homer's twisted logic making it even more absurd

jokes.text: 'The public owns you for life, and when you're dead, you'll all be in commercials, dancing with vacuum cleaners.'
jokes.setup: Homer continues his court speech about celebrity ownership
jokes.punchline: The specific image of dead celebrities in vacuum cleaner commercials
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and prophetic satire of posthumous celebrity exploitation with a perfectly absurd specific image

jokes.text: Homer making crank call: 'Hello? Old lady from Titanic? You stink!' [giggles]
jokes.setup: Homer is calling celebrities despite the restraining order
jokes.punchline: His childish prank call to Gloria Stuart with the generic insult
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish inability to leave celebrities alone, with his crude reference to Gloria Stuart

jokes.text: Hollywood executive discussing Homer's script: 'It's about a killer-robot driving instructor that travels back in time for some reason.' 'I'm listening.' 'His best friend's a talking pie.' 'Sold! Howard, you've done it again.'
jokes.setup: Ron Howard pitches Homer's terrible script to a studio executive
jokes.punchline: The executive loves it once he hears about the talking pie, satirizing Hollywood's love of absurd concepts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hollywood satire showing how terrible ideas get greenlit, with the callback to Homer's script
